answer:
**San Hyan Tattvajnana, Chapter 1.**

The assignments are stated as follows:

> the *Rākṣasa* is the patron divinity of the blades that are the weapons of a king.[^1]

> The *Daitya* is the patron divinity of the blades that are the implements of farmers, such as a *luke* or a *pṛkul* (types of agricultural blades).[^1]

> The *Dānava* is the patron divinity of the blades that are the implements of a priest, such as a *pamiso*, *kartrī*, or *karayu* (types of ritual knives).[^1]

This is immediately restated and elaborated within the blacksmith simile:

> There is an illustrative simile, like that of a blacksmith. He works with iron. The tools are of many kinds: hammer, anvil, tongs, file. All of them are ultimately one with the iron. In the same way, the raw iron is made into a blade by the smith. The blades are of many kinds. There is a blade that is the weapon of a king. There is a blade that is the implement of a farmer. There is a blade that is the implement of a priest. All of them are known as blades. As long as the iron is sharp, it is known as a blade. Only their functions are different. The blade that belongs to the king, such as a *curik*, a *malalya*, or a *śaṅkuh* (types of daggers), is used as a means for battle, to take life. The blade that belongs to the farmer, such as a *luke* or a *prĕkul* (agricultural blades), is used as a means to produce a livelihood. The blade that belongs to the priest, such as a *pakisi*, *kartrī*, or *karayu* (ritual knives), is used as a means for trimming or cutting. But it is the blade that strikes good and evil. As for the tools, they do not participate in striking good and evil.[^2]

[^1]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, [p-cbb351](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-cbb351).

[^2]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, [p-9c664a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-9c664a).

args.query: rākṣasa daitya dānava blade OR knife OR weapon
"Rākṣasa" OR "Daitya" OR "Dānava" blacksmith OR smith OR hammer OR anvil OR file OR tongs OR iron
"patron divinity" blade OR knife OR weapon
